text.title: trunk-based-dev-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for trunk-based-dev-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Pattern code is illustrative — is_enabled("name") assumes a real provider; agent will leave a stub and forget to wire it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hard-coded True makes the flag always-on in CI and production. Use env vars (FLAGS_DEFAULT=off in CI) to enforce safety and grep for literal is_enabled(...True).

failure-mode.description: Branch-by-Abstraction snippet skips the cleanup phase ("delete LegacyPaymentProcessor"); agents drop the abstraction and never collapse it back, leaving permanent indirection.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Permanent indirection wastes compute and confuses future agents. Always file a cleanup task before merging the new implementation.

failure-mode.description: Dark-launch comparison code re-raises exceptions into production paths.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The dark path should never crash the current path. Wrap in try/except at all costs; the README example does, agents often skip it.
